description:
Anthropic's latest Sonnet 4.6 release quietly introduced programmatic tool calling; a feature that lets AI agents write code instead of JSON to invoke tools, slashing token usage by up to 98% while improving accuracy. I break down why this "code mode" approach outperforms traditional tool calling, how companies like Cloudflare and Anthropic are already implementing it, and why this could become the new industry standard just like MCP did. If you're building AI agents, this might be the most important under-the-radar upgrade of the year.

https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/programmatic-tool-calling
https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-context-engineering-for-ai-agents
https://claude.com/blog/improved-web-search-with-dynamic-filtering
https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol
https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/agent-skills/overview
https://claude.com/blog/equipping-agents-for-the-real-world-with-agent-skills
https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/advanced-tool-use
